data.title: A spokesperson for the UK Reform Party stated that technologies such as stablecoins can help prevent financial fraud.
data.description: Richard Tice, a spokesperson for the UK's Reform Party, stated that new technologies such as stablecoins and blockchain can help improve the traceability of fund flows, thereby preventing financial fraud to some extent. He pointed out that using technology to track, intercept, or liquidate suspicious fund flows could help improve the efficiency of combating fraud, and emphasized remaining open to such possibilities. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Australia Enhances Oversight of Private Credit Industry
data.description: Australia's corporate regulator is intensifying its oversight of the private credit sector by demanding more comprehensive data from funds on a weekly basis. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increased scrutiny on the $1.8 trillion industry globally. This move reflects a growing focus on the private credit market, which has seen significant expansion and attention from regulators worldwide. The enhanced supervision aims to ensure transparency and mitigate risks associated with the burgeoning industry.

data.title: OpenAI Expands Codex Capabilities with New Plugin System
data.description: OpenAI has announced a reset of Codex usage limits across all subscription plans, enhancing accessibility for users. According to NS3.AI, the company has also introduced a new plugin system aimed at broadening the application of Codex in daily workflows. The initial set of plugins integrates Codex with popular platforms such as Slack, Figma, Notion, and Gmail, allowing users to streamline their tasks and improve productivity.

data.title: China's LNG Imports Set to Reach Lowest Level Since 2018 Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: China's liquefied natural gas (LNG) imports are projected to fall to their lowest level since 2018 this month, according to ship-tracking data from Kpler. Bloomberg posted on X that the decline is attributed to a surge in prices driven by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, which has dampened demand. The geopolitical tensions have led to increased volatility in the energy markets, affecting China's import levels. Analysts suggest that the situation may continue to impact global LNG trade patterns, as countries reassess their energy strategies in response to fluctuating prices and supply uncertainties.

data.title: Majority of DeFi Projects Struggle to Generate Significant Revenue
data.description:
DefiLlama data reveals that among approximately 1,300 DeFi projects, a staggering 97.6% have failed to generate significant revenue. According to ChainCatcher, the income distribution exhibits a pronounced power-law pattern.

Stablecoin issuers and trading platforms dominate the top of the revenue list, with Tether generating $490 million, Circle $201 million, and Hyperliquid $61 million.

Additionally, only six protocols have reported revenues between $10 million and $50 million. Over the past 30 days, only 32 projects, accounting for just 2.4% of the total, have achieved revenues exceeding $2 million. The projects with the largest losses include the MEV protocol Kairos Timeboost, with a loss of approximately $200,000, and the options platform Hegic, with a loss of around $23,000.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank May Raise Rates in April, Economist Suggests
data.description: Recent data on output gaps and price trends have strengthened the case for Japan's central bank to raise interest rates in April, according to Kenji Yamamoto, an economist at Daiwa Securities. According to Jin10, Yamamoto noted that despite ongoing inflation, Japan's economic supply and demand dynamics remain resilient. He emphasized that even if inflation appears to temporarily ease, there is no clear reason to delay the normalization of monetary policy as long as underlying price trends remain unchanged. These data points will assist Japan's central bank in tightening monetary policy in April, while maintaining cautious judgment, he added.

data.title: Ethereum developers are not prioritizing the inclusion of framework transaction proposals; the Hegota upgrade focuses on censorship resistance.
data.description: Ethereum core developers have decided not to prioritize the "framework transactions" proposal in the upcoming Hegota upgrade. Supported by Vitalik Buterin, the proposal aims to improve user experience and enhance quantum resistance, but developers believe its high implementation complexity could impact the upgrade's progress. Hegota is expected to launch in the second half of 2026, and FOCIL has already been identified as a key feature to improve the network's censorship resistance. Developers stated that framework transactions will be marked as "under consideration" rather than mandatory, and its technical complexity and implementation impact will require further evaluation. (DL News)

data.title: Ark Invest Sells Large Holdings in Tech Stocks and Crypto ETFs
data.description: Ark Invest, led by Cathie Wood, executed significant sales of technology stocks and cryptocurrency ETFs on March 27. According to BlockBeats, the fund sold approximately 155,441 shares of Nvidia, valued at around $27.77 million, and 76,622 shares of Meta, worth approximately $45.58 million. Additionally, Ark Invest offloaded 408,000 shares of the Bitcoin ETF (ARKB) and nearly 200,000 shares of Bullish stock.

data.title: Uncertainty Surrounds U.S. Cryptocurrency Leadership as David Sacks' Term Ends
data.description: The future of U.S. cryptocurrency leadership remains uncertain following the conclusion of David Sacks' term as a government special advisor. According to Odaily, it is unclear whether a new head for cryptocurrency affairs will be appointed. Meanwhile, policies such as the CLARITY Act and strategic Bitcoin reserves continue to progress. It is also yet to be determined if Sacks will remain involved in related matters as co-chair of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology.

data.title: Coinbase's policy chief calls for reform of crypto tax rules, saying the current system is outdated.
data.description: Coinbase Chief Policy Officer Faryar Shirzad has called on U.S. lawmakers to reform cryptocurrency tax rules, arguing that the current system, which treats crypto assets as "property," is no longer adequate for the industry's development. He pointed out that under current rules, even paying gas fees or using stablecoins for everyday transactions can trigger tax obligations, requiring users to calculate cost bases and track profits and losses, increasing compliance burdens. Coinbase data shows a 34% year-over-year increase in tax-related customer service inquiries, and millions of 1099-DA forms are expected to be issued by 2025, many involving small transactions. Shirzad also stated that over 63% of users have a cost base recording gap, suggesting a minimum exemption for small transactions to reduce compliance complexity, and warned that failure to adjust the rules could impact U.S. competitiveness in the crypto space. (Cryptopolitan)

data.title: OpenAI resets Codex usage limits and launches plugin system.
data.description: Thibault Sottiaux, head of OpenAI Codex, announced that Codex usage limits for all plans have been reset to accommodate the newly launched plugin system and encourage user experience. The plugin functionality initially integrates commonly used developer tools such as Slack, Figma, Notion, and Gmail, allowing users to utilize Codex capabilities in a wider range of applications.

data.title: The United States has not yet decided whether to appoint a new head of crypto affairs.
data.description: According to crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett, with David Sacks' term as a special advisor to the government ending, it remains unclear whether the United States will appoint a new head of cryptocurrency affairs. Terrett points out that policies such as the CLARITY Act and the Strategic Bitcoin Reserve are still under development, and it is also unclear whether Sacks will continue to be involved in related matters as co-chair of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology.

data.title: Shizuoka Financial and Bank of Nagoya Consider Merger Talks
data.description: Shizuoka Financial Group and Bank of Nagoya are reportedly in discussions to merge their operations under a single holding company. Bloomberg posted on X, citing unnamed sources familiar with the matter. The potential consolidation aims to enhance their competitive edge and streamline operations amid a challenging financial environment. Both institutions are exploring the benefits of such a merger, which could lead to increased efficiency and a stronger market presence. Further details on the negotiations have not been disclosed, and it remains uncertain when a formal agreement might be reached.

data.title: Iranian Media Reports U.S.-Israeli Attack in Qom Leaves Six Dead
data.description: According to Jin10, Iranian sources reported on March 27 that a U.S.-Israeli attack targeted three residential buildings in the Pardisan district of Qom, Iran. The incident resulted in the deaths of six individuals. Further details regarding the attack and its implications remain unclear.

data.title: Rupee Faces Persistent Pressure as Nifty Outlook Remains Uncertain
data.description:
The Indian rupee continues to experience significant pressure in the financial markets. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing challenges faced by the currency amid global economic uncertainties. Meanwhile, the Nifty index's best-case scenario remains uncertain as market volatility persists.

Analysts are closely monitoring the situation, with the volatility gauge indicating potential fluctuations in the market. The rupee's performance is being influenced by various factors, including international trade dynamics and domestic economic policies.

Investors are advised to stay cautious as the market navigates through these turbulent times. The outlook for the Nifty index will largely depend on how these economic factors unfold in the coming days.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. Judge Grants Anthropic's Request for Preliminary Injunction
data.description:
A federal judge in San Francisco has approved Anthropic's request for a preliminary injunction. According to Foresight News, this decision comes after Anthropic filed a lawsuit against the Trump administration, challenging the Pentagon's decision to blacklist the company and U.S. President Donald Trump's directive prohibiting federal agencies from using its Claude model. Anthropic sought the injunction to halt these actions and prevent further economic and reputational harm during the legal proceedings.

Judge Rita Lin issued the ruling on Thursday, describing the government's ban as "troubling" and suggesting it appeared to be an attempt to undermine Anthropic.

data.title: Peter Brandt: Bitcoin may be forming a rising wedge sell signal
data.description: Analyst Peter Brandt wrote on the X platform that Bitcoin's current price action may be forming an ascending wedge pattern, potentially signaling a sell opportunity.

data.title: Polysilicon Prices in China Decline Amid Weak Market Sentiment
data.description: Polysilicon prices in China have decreased for the fourth consecutive week, reflecting ongoing bearish sentiment regarding supply and demand dynamics. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the continued pressure on the market as producers and consumers navigate the current economic landscape. The decline in prices underscores the challenges faced by the industry, as market participants remain cautious about future prospects.

data.title: Innovent's Shares Surge Following First Annual Profit Announcement
data.description: Innovent Biologics experienced its largest share increase in almost five months after reporting its first annual profit. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the Chinese pharmaceutical company attributed this financial success to the rising demand for its weight-loss medication. The positive financial results have significantly boosted investor confidence, leading to a notable rise in the company's stock value.

data.title: The crypto market saw a broad correction, with the AI ​​sector falling nearly 8% and BTC dropping below $69,000.
data.description: On March 27th, according to SoSoValue data, renewed negotiation deadlock reignited market panic, leading to a general pullback in the crypto sector. The AI ​​sector performed poorly, falling 7.96% in the past 24 hours. Among them, Siren (SIREN), which had previously seen consecutive gains, fell 29.88%, while Worldcoin (WLD) and Kite (KITE) fell 8.81% and 16.60% respectively. Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) fell 3.17%, dropping below $69,000; Ethereum (ETH) fell 4.21%, dropping below $2,100. In other sectors, the CeFi sector fell 2.20% in the last 24 hours, but Aster (ASTER) rose 1.35%; the PayFi sector fell 2.88%, and XRP (XRP) fell 3.16%; the Layer 1 sector fell 3.26%, with Canton Network (CC) rising 2.53% intraday; the DeFi sector fell 3.77%, with Onyxcoin (XCN) relatively resilient, rising 5.34%; the Layer 2 sector fell 4.04%, with OpenLedger (OPEN) bucking the trend and rising 8%; the Meme sector fell 5.14%, with BUILDon (B) rising 2.14% intraday. Crypto sector indices reflecting historical sector performance show that the ssiSocialFi, ssiAI, and ssiMeme indices fell 5.25%, 5.11%, and 4.17%, respectively.
